title: 10 best herbal tinctures for dry lips

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with dry lips because of its remarkable emollient and anti-inflammatory properties. The tincture's potent flavonoids and carotenoids work to soothe and moisturize the skin, providing long-lasting relief from dryness and chapping. Additionally, pot marigold's natural antiseptic qualities help to reduce redness and irritation, leaving lips feeling soft, smooth, and refreshed. With regular use, this herbal remedy can provide a healthy and radiant appearance for your lips.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce redness and swelling in the lips, providing relief from dry, irritated skin.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of pot marigold tincture help protect the lips from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness and flakiness, promoting healthier lip tissue.
Emollient: Pot marigold's emollient properties help moisturize and soothe dry, chapped lips by forming a protective barrier on the skin's surface, locking in moisture and reducing further irritation.
remedy_constituents: Beta-sitosterol: Helps with dry lips by reducing inflammation and promoting skin regeneration, thereby soothing and protecting dry, chapped skin.

Sesquiterpenes (including calendulin): Help with dry lips by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which aid in healing and protecting the skin from external irritants and infections.
Saponins: Aid in soothing and moisturizing dry lips by forming a protective barrier on the skin's surface, locking in moisture and reducing water loss.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in calendulin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them ideal for treating dry, chapped lips.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that help to protect and moisturize the skin, reducing dryness and irritation of the lips.
Buds: They possess similar anti-inflammatory and soothing properties as the flowers, making them a suitable alternative for treating dry, cracked lips.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup of fresh calendula officinalis flowers and chop them into small pieces.
Combine the chopped flowers with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks, shaking jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Store the final herbal tincture in a cool dark place for at least 4 weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with dry lips because of its unique ability to provide instant hydration and nourishment. The aloe vera plant contains vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that work together to soothe and moisturize the skin. When applied topically as an herbal tincture, these beneficial compounds help to lock in moisture, reducing dryness and flakiness on the lips. As a result, lips become soft, supple, and protected from further irritation, making aloe tinctures a natural and effective remedy for dry, chapped lips.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation caused by chapped lips, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing dry, cracked lips.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial agents in aloe vera tinctures inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause infections and further dry out the lips, promoting healthy lip tissue and preventing infection.

Moisturizing and emollient properties: Aloe vera's moisturizing compounds help to lock in moisture, soothe and calm dry, chapped lips, and restore their natural barrier function, leaving them feeling soft, smooth, and supple.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin is a lignan, a type of phenolic compound, that helps with dry lips by reducing inflammation and soothing irritation in the skin.
Catechins: Catechins, a type of flavonoid, help to protect the skin from oxidative stress and promote hydration, thereby alleviating dryness and discomfort on the lips.
Aloin: Aloin, a glycoside and anthraquinone derivative, has anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that aid in hydrating and soothing dry, chapped lips.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Aloe vera due to their high concentration of soothing compounds like aloe-emodin, which helps to moisturize and protect dry lips.

Gel (from Leaves): Although it's a derivative of leaves, the gel is so commonly used that I'll add it: The gel from Aloe vera leaves is used to make tinctures because it contains a high percentage of water and glycoproteins that help to hydrate and soothe dry, chapped lips.

Flowers: The flowers of the Aloe vera plant are used in tinctures for dry lips because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce lip irritation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest aloe vera leaves with a sharp knife by cutting them from the plant at the base of the leaf.
Dry the leaves in a single layer on paper towels for 7 to 10 days in a warm dry place.
Chop the dried leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife with a 1 part aloe vera to 2 parts solvent ratio.
Steep the chopped leaves in a high-proof alcohol solvent like everclear or vodka for 2 to 3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and store it in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curry plant tinctures helps with dry lips because they possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and calm chapped lips. The tincture's extracts, such as curcuminoids and terpenes, penetrate deep into the skin to provide hydration and reduce inflammation. As a result, dry, flaky lips become soft and supple, with improved texture and appearance. Additionally, the tincture's antimicrobial properties help to prevent infection and promote healthy lip tissue, leaving lips feeling smooth and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Curry plant tincture's antioxidant compounds help protect the lips from environmental stressors and damage, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture's anti-inflammatory agents soothe and calm irritated, dry lips by reducing redness and swelling, providing relief from discomfort and pain.
Emollient and moisturizing properties: Curry plant tincture contains emollients that help lock in moisture, soften, and hydrate dry, chapped lips, leaving them feeling smooth and supple.
remedy_constituents: Neryl Acetate: This terpene component helps with dry lips by reducing inflammation and promoting skin regeneration, thereby soothing and moisturizing dry, chapped lips.

Italidione: This sesquiterpene lactone helps with dry lips by reducing oxidative stress and promoting antioxidant activity, which helps protect the skin from damage and promote healing of dry, cracked lips.

Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene helps with dry lips by reducing inflammation and promoting anti-irritant activity, which helps soothe and calm dry, irritated skin and promote healing of dry, chapped lips.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and moisturize dry lips.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their emollient and soothing properties, which help to hydrate and calm dry, chapped lips.
Stems: The stems are used for their astringent properties, which help to tighten and protect dry skin on the lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of helichrysum italicum flowers and leaves at dawn in july and august.
Chop 1 part of the harvested plant material into small pieces using a sharp knife.
Combine 3 parts of 80 proof vodka with 1 part of the chopped plant material in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks to allow extraction to occur slowly.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar and discard the solids immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tinctures helps with dry lips because it possesses anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that soothe and hydrate the skin. The herb's rich source of allantoin, a natural emollient, deeply nourishes and protects the lip tissue, reducing inflammation and flakiness. Additionally, comfrey's antiseptic qualities help to eliminate bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to dryness and discomfort. As a result, comfrey tinctures provide long-lasting relief from dry, chapped lips, leaving them soft, smooth, and healthy-looking.
remedy_properties: Allantoin content: Comfrey tincture contains allantoin, a natural humectant that helps retain moisture in the skin and lips, providing relief from dryness and cracking.
Anti-inflammatory activity: The tannins present in comfrey tincture have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with dry, chapped lips.

Wound healing support: Comfrey's high levels of rosmarinic acid and other bioactive compounds may help accelerate wound healing by promoting collagen synthesis, tissue repair, and reducing inflammation, making it beneficial for the treatment of lip cracks and fissures.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids (specifically, Symphytine and Symphytinine): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to reduce dryness and irritation of the lips.

Flavonoids (specifically, Quercetin and Kaempferol): These flavonoids possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the lips from damage, reduce inflammation, and promote healthy tissue regeneration.

Saponins (specifically, Symphytum saponin): These compounds have moisturizing and protective properties, which help to lock in moisture, soothe dryness, and shield the lips from environmental stressors.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of mucilage, a thick, soothing substance that helps to lock in moisture and protect dry, chapped lips.
Roots: Roots are also frequently used for their rich content of mucilage and other bioactive compounds that promote skin health and hydration.

Flowers: Flowers are used for their delicate, yet potent, mucilage content, which is thought to be gentler on the skin than the roots or leaves while still providing soothing and moisturizing benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh symphytum officinale roots and leaves in late summer or early fall.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to increase the surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tinctures helps with dry lips because of its remarkable moisturizing and soothing properties. The plantain herb contains mucilages, which are naturally occurring lubricants that help to lock in moisture and protect the delicate skin on the lips from further drying out. When applied topically as a tincture, plantain helps to calm irritated lip tissue and provide long-lasting relief from dryness, itchiness, and discomfort. Additionally, plantain's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to reduce redness and swelling associated with chapped or cracked lips.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Plantain tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with dry, chapped lips.
Moisturizing property: The mucilages present in plantain tincture can help lock in moisture and soothe dry, cracked lips by forming a protective barrier on the skin's surface.

Antioxidant property: Plantain's antioxidant compounds can neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and skin damage, promoting healthy lip tissue and reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Plantago major tinctures help soothe and moisturize dry lips by creating a protective barrier that locks in moisture and shields against external irritants.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin and kaempferol): These flavonoids exhibit anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and redness associated with dry, chapped lips, promoting faster healing and relief.

Mucilages (specifically, mucilage polysaccharides): Mucilages in Plantago major tinctures form a protective, soothing film on the skin that helps retain moisture, calm irritation, and reduce discomfort associated with dry, chapped lips.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain mucilages, which provide soothing and moisturizing properties to help relieve dry and chapped lips.
Seeds: They are rich in fatty acids, which can help to lock in moisture and provide nourishment to dry lips.
Roots: They contain a variety of bioactive compounds that may help to soothe and protect dry, irritated skin, including dry lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves in the early morning to ensure highest concentration of medicinal properties.
Clean the leaves thoroughly with distilled water to remove any dirt and debris.
Combine 1 part plantago major leaves with 2 parts vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks to allow extraction to occur.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a separate container to remove the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with dry lips because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herb contains apigenin, an antioxidant that calms irritated skin and reduces redness, making it an effective remedy for chapped and dry lips. Additionally, chamomile's moisturizing properties help to lock in hydration and protect the lip surface from further drying out. When applied topically as a tincture, chamomile's benefits can provide quick relief from dry, cracked lips, leaving them feeling soft, smooth, and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, can reduce swelling and redness associated with chapped or dry lips.
Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidants can help protect the skin from environmental stressors and free radicals that can contribute to lip dryness and damage.
Soothing and calming effects: Chamomile's flavonoids, such as apigenin, have a sedative effect on the skin, which can help calm irritated or inflamed lips, reducing discomfort and promoting healing.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and soothing irritation associated with dry lips.
Alpha-bisabolol: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties, helping to calm and hydrate dry, chapped lips.
Luteolin: This flavonoid has antioxidant properties, protecting the skin from oxidative stress and promoting the healing of dry, cracked lips.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm dry and chapped lips.
Leaves: They are used due to their antiseptic and antifungal properties, which help to prevent infections and promote healing of dry lips.

Seeds: They are not typically used as much for tinctures, however, I was forced to select only 3. The actual most used part is the Flowers: They are used due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm dry and chapped lips, but I was forced to select only 3.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and 750ml of 40% glycerin or ethanol as the solvent.
Chop the dried flowers into small pieces to increase their surface area for better extraction.
Combine the chopped flowers with the solvent in a clean glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard the solids.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and label it clearly with the date and contents.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with dry lips because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The tincture's glycyrrhizin content helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing, making it an effective remedy for chapped and cracked lips. Additionally, licorice tinctures provide antioxidants that help protect the lips from environmental stressors and moisturize the skin, leaving lips feeling soft, smooth, and hydrated.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice root's anti-inflammatory property helps to soothe and calm dry, irritated lips by reducing redness and swelling.
Moisturizing: The saponins in licorice root provide a natural moisturizing effect, helping to lock in moisture and hydrate dry, chapped lips.
Antioxidant: Licorice's antioxidant properties help protect the lips from environmental stressors and free radicals that can cause dryness and damage, promoting healthy lip tissue.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These triterpenoid glycosides help to lock in moisture and protect the skin from further dryness, thereby reducing the discomfort associated with dry lips.
Glycyrrhizin: This primary active compound is known to exhibit anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to calm and comfort dry, chapped lips.

Flavonoids: These phenolic compounds possess antioxidant properties that help to protect the skin from free radical damage, promote collagen production, and maintain the skin's natural barrier function, all of which can contribute to healthier, more hydrated lips.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are commonly used due to their high glycyrrhizin content, which has anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that help soothe dry lips.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect and hydrate dry lips.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help calm and moisturize dry lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest glycyrrhiza glabra roots in late summer or early fall when the plant is mature.
Clean the roots thoroughly with water to remove dirt and debris before drying.
Dry the roots in a well-ventilated area at a temperature below 40 degrees celsius for 2 weeks.
Crush 100 grams of dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Steep 100 grams of powdered roots in 500 milliliters of 95 percent ethanol for 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St john's wort tinctures helps with dry lips because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The herb's antimicrobial actions also help to combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause dryness and discomfort. Additionally, St John's Wort contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which provide antioxidant protection and help to lock in moisture, leaving lips feeling soft, smooth, and hydrated. Regular use of a St John's Wort tincture can help to alleviate dry lip symptoms and promote overall lip health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St. John's Wort tincture contains antioxidants that help protect the lips from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness and cracking.
Anti-inflammatory properties: St. John's Wort has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the lips, which can exacerbate dryness and discomfort.

Wound-healing and soothing properties: The flavonoids and terpenes present in St. John's Wort tincture have been shown to promote wound healing and soothe irritated skin, making it an effective remedy for dry, chapped lips.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Acts as a potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, helping to soothe and protect dry, irritated lips by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Flavonoids (including rutin and quercetin): Function as antioxidants and free radical scavengers, which may help to protect and moisturize dry lips by neutralizing damage-causing free radicals and promoting collagen production.

Naphthodianthrones (including hypericin and pseudohypericin): Display anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in reducing inflammation, preventing infection, and promoting the healing of dry, cracked lips.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which provide antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties to soothe dry lips.
Flowers: Contain hyperforin, a compound that helps to protect and moisturize dry, chapped lips.
Leaves: Also contain glycosides and other compounds that aid in wound healing and tissue repair, beneficial for dry, cracked lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of fresh st johns wort flowers and leaves on a dry morning when dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer for 7 to 10 days at 35 to 45 degrees celsius.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces and combine it with 750 milliliters of 35% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tinctures helps with dry lips because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The tannins present in stinging nettle have astringent properties that help to reduce lip inflammation and tighten the skin, providing instant relief from dryness and chapping. Additionally, stinging nettle's antioxidants and vitamins A and E promote healthy skin cell growth, further nourishing and hydrating dry lips. This natural remedy provides long-lasting results without leaving any sticky residue or artificial fragrances behind.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Nettle tincture helps reduce inflammation and swelling on the lips, relieving discomfort and promoting healing.
Antioxidant activity: Nettle's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals that can damage lip tissues, protecting against dryness and chapping.
Soothing and emollient effects: Nettle's mucilages and saponins create a protective barrier on the lips, locking in moisture and soothing irritation, reducing discomfort and promoting softness.
remedy_constituents: Ascorbic acid: This vitamin C compound helps to hydrate and moisturize the skin, reducing dryness and inflammation associated with chapped lips.
Polysaccharides and mucilages: These complex carbohydrates form a protective barrier on the skin, locking in moisture and providing soothing relief to dry, irritated lips.
Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and discomfort caused by dry, cracked lips.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe dry, irritated skin.
Roots: The roots contain compounds that promote skin health and have been used to treat skin conditions, including dryness and irritation.
Stems: The stems have anti-inflammatory properties and contain compounds that help to soothe and protect dry, chapped skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/3 to 1/2 of the plant tops and leaves of urtica dioica in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested urtica dioica plant material in a warm low-heat oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine 1 part dried urtica dioica plant material with 2 parts 95 grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Shake the jar containing the urtica dioica and alcohol mixture daily for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discard the solids and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with dry lips because its mucilaginous properties provide a soothing, protective barrier for the skin. The tincture's botanical ingredients, derived from the marshmallow plant, create a gentle, non-irritating coating that locks in moisture and shields the lips from harsh environmental factors. As the tincture absorbs into the skin, it helps to soften and hydrate dry, chapped lips, leaving them feeling smooth, supple, and comforted.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow tincture contains mucilages, which create a protective film on the skin to lock in moisture and soothe dry, chapped lips, reducing inflammation and discomfort.

Antioxidants: The antioxidant properties of marshmallow tincture help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the lips, promoting healthy lip tissue and reducing dryness and irritation.

Soothing and anti-inflammatory compounds: Marshmallow tincture contains a range of soothing and anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and saponins, which help to calm inflammation and reduce redness and pain associated with dry, chapped lips.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help to soothe and protect dry, chapped lips by forming a protective barrier on the skin surface, reducing irritation and promoting healing.

Flavonoids (such as quercetin and kaempferol): Flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in dry, cracked lips, promoting healthy tissue repair and regeneration.

Phenolic acids (such as ferulic acid and vanillic acid): Phenolic acids exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help to neutralize free radicals, reduce inflammation, and promote the healing of dry, irritated skin on the lips.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used for tinctures due to their high mucilage content, which provides soothing and moisturizing properties for dry lips.
Leaves: The leaves are also used for tinctures, as they contain a similar high concentration of mucilage, making them effective in relieving dry, chapped, and irritated skin, including lips.
Barks: The barks of Althaea officinalis contain mucilage and other compounds that help to soothe and protect dry, cracked skin, making them another commonly used part for tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh or 4 cups of dried roots of the plant in early spring or late summer.
Clean the roots thoroughly by gently scrubbing them with a soft brush under cold running water.
Chop the roots into small pieces and combine with 2 cups of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with dry lips because of its remarkable moisturizing properties. The herb's anti-inflammatory compounds soothe and calm chapped, cracked skin on the lips, while its antioxidants protect against environmental damage that can exacerbate dryness. Seeds: Containing omega-3 fatty acids and vitamins, dandelion seeds can provide nourishing hydration and protection for dry lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers at peak potency and quality for optimal tincture production.
Combine 200g of dried taraxacum officinale herb with 400ml of 95% ethanol in a glass jar for maceration.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow for gentle extraction and infusion.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container discarding the solids after 10 minutes.
Transfer the clear tincture to amber glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and label with date of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with dry lips because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The tannins present in fennel help to tighten the skin's natural barrier, reducing moisture loss and preventing further dryness. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried foeniculum vulgare leaves in quantities sufficient for the desired batch size.
Chop the leaves into small pieces and combine with vodka or other high-proof spirit in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks to allow for extraction and infusion.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth and discard the solids after 2-3 hours of preparation time.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store in the refrigerator at 40°f for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram tinctures helps with dry lips because of its natural soothing properties that calm and moisturize the skin. remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, the flowers of wild marjoram have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and moisturize dry lips.
Roots: Containing natural emollients and humectants, the roots of wild marjoram provide a gentle, non-irritating solution for dry, chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality oregano leaves measuring 1 cup fresh or 2 tablespoons dried.
Cleanse the oregano leaves by rinsing them gently with filtered water and patting dry.
Combine the cleaned oregano leaves with 1 cup of 95% ethanol or vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 14 days, shaking the jar daily, at room temperature.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding solids and storing in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tinctures helps with dry lips because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's natural oils help to lock in moisture, soothing and calming dry, chapped lips. Additionally, rosemary's antimicrobial properties help to prevent the growth of bacteria that can exacerbate lip dryness. Leaves: Rosemary leaves contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with dry lips, while also providing a natural barrier against environmental irritants.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves in late summer or early fall for optimal potency.
Cut the harvested leaves into small pieces and measure out exactly 50 grams of material per 100 milliliters of liquid.
Combine the cut rosmarinis officinalis leaves with 100 milliliters of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar with a lid.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow for maximum extraction and infusion of active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids and label as a 1:1 rosmarinus officinalis tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with dry lips because of its natural moisturizing properties. The herb's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds work to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and redness associated with dry lips. As a result, lemon balm tincture helps to lock in moisture, leaving lips feeling soft, smooth, and hydrated. Additionally, the herb's antimicrobial properties help to prevent infection and promote healthy lip tissue growth, ensuring long-lasting relief from dry, chapped lips.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm's antioxidant compounds help protect the lips from environmental stressors and oxidative damage, which can contribute to dryness and chapping.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in lemon balm tincture have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with dry, irritated lips.
Mouth-soothing compounds: Lemon balm contains mucilages, such as rosmarinic acid, which are naturally soothing to the mouth tissues, helping to calm and moisturize dry, chapped lips.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots are valued for their ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, making them an excellent choice for dry lip treatment.
Seeds: Lemon balm seeds contain oils that help to lock in moisture and provide relief from dry, chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves at peak potency during early summer morning dew.
Chop 1 part dried melissa officinalis leaves and mix with 2 parts vodka in glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 4 weeks shaking jar daily, then strain through cheesecloth into clean bottle.
Add glycerin to the tincture at a ratio of 10% to enhance lip moisturizing properties and store in dark cool place.
Shake the tincture well before use and apply directly to dry lips 2-3 times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Creeping thyme tinctures helps with dry lips because of its natural ability to soothe and moisturize the skin. The herbal extract contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and irritation caused by dryness. Additionally, creeping thyme's antibacterial and antifungal properties can help to prevent infection and promote healthy healing of cracked or chapped lips. By applying a few drops of creeping thyme tincture to the affected area, you can experience instant relief from dry, uncomfortable lips.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial compounds help to combat yeast and bacteria on the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing of dry, cracked lips.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and terpenes in thyme tincture reduce swelling and redness associated with chapped lips, providing relief from discomfort and pain.
Soothing and moisturizing properties: Thyme's saponins help to lock in moisture and soothe dry skin, leaving lips feeling soft, smooth, and protected.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the creeping thyme plant contain a high concentration of antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to soothe and moisturize dry lips.
Flowers: The delicate flowers of the creeping thyme plant possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness and irritation associated with dry lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh thymus serpyllum leaves and flowers from your local pharmacy or supplier on a sunny day in july.
Cleanse the plant material by gently rinsing it with distilled water in a fine-mesh strainer to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine the cleaned plant material with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring that the plant material is completely submerged.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar twice daily to allow the herbs to steep and extract their active compounds.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and leaving behind a final product containing 20% thymus serpyllum extract in vodka.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry tinctures helps with dry lips because of its unique combination of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. The tannins present in highbush cranberries have astringent properties that help to dry up excess moisture on the skin, providing instant relief from chapped and cracked lips. Additionally, the antiseptic properties of highbush cranberry help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection, promoting healthy lip tissue and leaving your lips feeling soft, smooth, and hydrated.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Highbush cranberry tincture's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to dry and chapped lips.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in highbush cranberry tincture can help to soothe and calm irritated skin on the lips, reducing redness and swelling associated with dryness.

Moisturizing properties: Highbush cranberry contains a high water content and has been traditionally used as a natural emollient, making it an effective remedy for locking in moisture and softening dry, cracked lips.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and moisturizing dry lips.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit emollient and humectant properties, helping to lock in moisture for dry lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh vaccinium macrocarpon berries in the summer months for optimal potency and freshness.
Clean and chop 250g of fresh vaccinium macrocarpon berries into small pieces to facilitate extraction.
Combine chopped vaccinium macrocarpon berries with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring complete coverage.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking occasionally to facilitate extraction and infusion.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids and labeling for future use.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for dry lips are concentrated liquid extracts made from plants and herbs that have natural moisturizing and soothing properties. These tinctures help to alleviate dry, chapped, and cracked lips by providing intense hydration and nourishment. Examples of herbal tinctures that benefit dry lips include calendula, aloe vera, and chamomile, which can be applied topically to provide long-lasting relief. By using these tinctures, individuals with dry lips can experience improved comfort, reduced irritation, and a softer, more supple appearance, allowing them to enjoy life without the discomfort of dry, chapped lips.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dry lips that are treated with herbal tinctures are skin irritations such as eczema, psoriasis, and rosacea. Herbal tinctures like calendula, chamomile, and tea tree oil can soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Additionally, tinctures of marshmallow root and aloe vera can provide relief from minor burns, cuts, and scrapes by promoting tissue repair and wound closure.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with dry lips is a blend of aloe vera, chamomile, and honey. Aloe vera provides soothing hydration, while chamomile calms inflammation and reduces irritation. Honey's antibacterial and moisturizing properties further nourish and protect dry lips. When combined in equal parts, these tinctures create a natural remedy to combat dryness, itchiness, and chapping, promoting healthy and soft lip tissue. Apply topically as needed for optimal results.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Forschende Komplementarmedizin und klassische Naturheilkunde = Research in complementary and natural classical medicine, herbal tinctures are good for dry lips because they contain antibacterial properties. The study found that alcohol-based herbal tinctures, such as those used in PADMA 28, showed an improved inhibitory effect on Gram-positive bacteria in vitro (MIC: 0.38-1.51% tincture or 0.38-1.51 mg PADMA 28/ml). This suggests that the antibacterial properties of herbal tinctures can help to combat the bacterial infections that often cause dry, cracked lips.
